nativeRange.habitat: Found in shallow coastal reef zones and lagoons within the photic zone, growing on reef substrate in shallow subtidal conditions typical of the genus.

decorPreferences: loose placement on rockwork or rubble

avoidWith: heavily grazing herbivores that may consume the specimen before it establishes

summary: A red macroalgae with distinctive flat, plate-like branching and striped patterning, Gracilaria mammaralis is a specialty species suited to refugium use and reef display, growing readily under standard reef parameters given moderate light and flow.
careNotes:
The Flat-Branched Striped Gracilaria (Gracilaria mammaralis) is a red macroalga (Rhodophyta) in the family Gracilariaceae — the same family as other commonly kept Gracilaria species in the hobby. Its distinguishing feature is a flat, plate-like branching growth form with striped patterning that develops as the specimen matures, in contrast to the rounder, more cylindrical branching of species like Red Ogo (Gracilaria parvispora) or the bushy ball-form of Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi).

Dedicated husbandry documentation for this specific species is sparse, but care can be guided confidently by the well-established parameters shared across the genus. Temperature is generally advised in the range of 22–26°C (72–78°F), salinity 1.023–1.025 SG, pH 8.1–8.4, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH — standard reef parameters suitable for all Gracilariaceae species in the trade.

Lighting is expected to be medium intensity, consistent with other Gracilaria refugium species, and growth rate tends to be fast in keeping with the genus. The flat branching form suggests it may be more decorative than purely utilitarian compared to tumbling refugium species; however, like other Gracilaria, it can be expected to provide meaningful nitrate and phosphate export when grown under adequate light and flow.

As with all Gracilaria, herbivorous fish such as tangs and rabbitfish are likely to graze it. Whether this is desirable (as a source of supplemental live food) or a concern (for display specimens) depends on the goals of the system. Copper-based medications should not be used in any aquarium containing macroalgae.

As a red alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand or sediment — it should be attached to rockwork rather than placed in a sand bed. Suitable attachment methods include superglue gel, zip-tie or fishing line, or simple wedging into rockwork crevices.
breedingNotes: In captivity, propagation is achieved through simple fragmentation: portions of the thallus that detach or are trimmed will generally establish new growth when placed in adequate flow and lighting. Sexual reproduction via alternation of gametophyte and sporophyte generations occurs in the wild but is not a documented propagation path in the hobby.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirms scientific name Gracilaria mammaralis and flat plate-like branching growth form with striped patterning. No water parameter or numeric husbandry data provided on product page.

additional.name: Sibling species cross-reference (Red Ogo, Pom Pom, Gracilariaceae genus-level)
additional.notes: Water parameters (temperatureC, pH, kH, salinity) extrapolated from established sibling-species data for Gracilaria parvispora (sw-algae-007) and Gracilaria hayi (sw-algae-006), both Gracilariaceae. Parameters are standard reef values confirmed across multiple trade sources for the genus.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
